cv

Basics

Name LEE MULLINS
Label Data Engineer & Biomedical Informatics Specialist
Email lee.ellen.mullins@gmail.com
Phone 706-400-9302
Summary Data engineer and biomedical informatics specialist focused on building observable, containerized data pipelines for medical imaging. Experienced in QC gating logic, high-integrity data transformations, and HIPAA/IRB-compliant workflows. Passionate about reproducible research and cross-functional collaboration in complex data environments.

Work

  • 2024.09 - Present
    Data Analyst
    Emory University School of Medicine
    • Developed reproducible pipelines for MRI data, enabling scalable infant health research.
    • Created tools for structural MRI QC (bias correction, skull stripping), packaging workflows for team-wide reuse.
    • Converted DICOM to BIDS with strict metadata enforcement and PHI scrubbing; ensured NIH/NDA audit readiness.
    • Standardized cross-lab datasets by implementing clear directory structures, automated CSV generation, and permissioned access to reduce confusion and data loss.
    • Bridged communication gaps across researchers, postdocs, and IT by writing documentation, training users, and managing updates.
    • Advocated for better workflow planning, building systems to track dataset progress and support cross-functional collaboration.

Volunteer

  • 2025.02 - 2025.02
    STEM Outreach Speaker
    Local Middle School
    7th Grade STEM Session
    • Led an interactive talk on proteins, genomics, brain imaging, and AI, including a demo on how gene mutations affect phenotype and a Q&A on future tech and medicine.
  • 2025 - 2025
    Speaker
    Gwinnett County Public Library
    What Is AI, Really?
    • Introductory talk for parents and community members on how AI works, what it can and can't do, and its growing role in education, work, and daily life.

Education

  • 2022.08 - 2024.08

    Atlanta, Georgia

    M.S.
    Georgia Institute of Technology
    Bioinformatics
    • Computational Genomics
    • Machine Learning
    • Data Structures
    • Databases

Projects

  • 2024 - Present
    Salesforce Agentforce Hackathon
    Designing a GenAI-powered agent to monitor CRM data quality, detect anomalies, and suggest standardizations.
    • Uses approval-based automation to maintain governance and trust.
    • In Progress
  • 2024 - 2024
    BIDS MRI QC Pipeline
    Built a modular QC pipeline for structural/functional MRI in BIDS format.
    • Included automated preprocessing, a web-based annotation tool, gated validation, and HTML report generation.
  • 2024 - 2024
    DICOM-to-BIDS Conversion Package
    Developed a pip-installable tool to convert raw DICOM to BIDS-compliant datasets.
    • PHI scrubbing, JSON validation, and structured logging.
  • 2024 - 2024
    Cell Classification Pipeline on AWS
    Deployed a secure cloud-based inference pipeline for single-cell classification.
    • Enabled OAuth2-authenticated access and logged results to DynamoDB for scalable team use.
